segments.author: Automatic_Aioli8500
segments.text:
Parking?

I wasn’t awarded a parking pass this quarter. I am a single mother who commutes from the valley. Does anyone have any suggestions on where I can park? Thank you so much :)

segments.author: itwontmendyourheart
segments.text: For free parking, the UA neighborhood (Westwood Hills North encompassing frat row + UA apartments) is your best bet, however it will be a walk to main campus (the same walk lots of undergrads including myself have to take, so it is what it is). Someone posted a visual guide to the Westwood Hills North area I am referencing for free parking, including sweep days and time limits. You can also just buy daily passes in certain structures if you need them for a day. If you're able to stack your classes on certain days that might help you in the future since parking for commuters is limited (BS btw). I have lived in this neighborhood all my years at UCLA and have never *not* been able to find a spot, but depending on the day it can be extremely limited so I would plan to leave early. Eventually you'll get a feel for it. **Edited to fix link

segments.author: biggamehaunter
segments.text: I would say those street parking is EXTREMELY limited....

segments.author: biggamehaunter
segments.text: That's crazy. Can't we shop at the store and also visit somewhere nearby...

segments.author: RevolutionarySea15
segments.text: If you go to the upper level parking lot attached to Whole Foods - the one where you have to actually get a ticket to enter - they have a day rate if you want to park more than an hour or two. There is no one hour limit on that lot but they do give you an hour free if you validate your parking stub at Whole Foods. Otherwise there is a parking lot on (I think) Le Conte and Gayley. You pay with an app so it's easy. And from there it's a manageable walk to campus if you just give yourself enough time.

segments.author: Just_Ur_Avg_Teen
segments.text: DO NOT DO THIS. I tried it a single time for my student job (for only 2 hrs) and I got towed. Ended costing me upwards of $400 to get it back from the impound lot in Beverly Hills.

segments.author: iamtherepairman
segments.text: Ucla is garbage with this. In 2002, I parked at a Metro train station and rode city bus for hours to go to class. I read on the bus. Ucla doesn't care. Meanwhile they recently let some Middle East oil rich prince park right by the flag pole near Royce and Powell. Don't donate to this school. I have not and will not.
